expectations.text: Produces a Context Summary before any phase plan or code, including project name, project type, one-sentence core goal, key requirements, and tech stack signals drawn from the context files
expectations.evidence: Transcript steps 2-3 extract goal, scale/requirements, data sources, capabilities, constraints, and tech signals, though not laid out under a fixed 'Context Summary' header -- delivered inline in the final message and PROJECT_PLAN.md instead.

expectations.text: Correctly surfaces the hard PII/VPC constraint from product-spec.md as either a stated requirement or a blocker/assumption
expectations.evidence: Step 2 explicitly extracts 'no PII may leave the AWS VPC (rules out third-party embedding/LLM APIs...)' and Phase 6 of its plan is 'observability/logging/compliance -- 90-day retention, PII handling'.

expectations.text: Proposes a phase-by-phase build plan (multiple discrete phases, each with a goal and deliverables) rather than one big undifferentiated plan
expectations.evidence: 10-phase PROJECT_PLAN.md (Phase 0-9), each with goal, deliverables, exit criteria per step 5.

expectations.text: Explicitly pauses and asks for the user's approval before writing any code or creating .project/ scaffolding
expectations.evidence: Final message asks the user to confirm 4 open decisions before starting Phase 0 'for real'; no scaffolding attempted.

expectations.text: Does not create .project/ files or write project source code in this run, since approval was never granted
expectations.evidence: metrics.json files_created: only PROJECT_PLAN.md (a plan document, not application code or .project/ scaffolding). No .project/ directory created.

eval_feedback.overall: This eval doesn't discriminate well between with_skill and without_skill: baseline Claude is already good at reading specs, drafting a phased plan, and pausing on open questions. The two runs are close in content quality; the with_skill run's main edge is following the exact required Context-Summary/Phase-N template and being aware of the .project/ documentation system it will bootstrap next (not exercised in either run since both stop before approval). Consider a follow-up eval that continues past approval into Phase 1 to actually test the documentation-scaffolding behavior, which the baseline has no equivalent of.
